metadata.title: Private credit under zero-trust - manager-marked NAVs ('mark-to-myth'), the AI-buildout's marginal lender, and the insurance/retail channel that absorbs the risk
metadata.frame: The THIRD self-marked asset class, completing the pattern with bank HTM (carried at cost) and AI stakes (fair-value/equity marks). Private-credit loans are valued by the MANAGER (who earns fees on the mark), are illiquid, and are not exchange-priced - so reported NAVs are a chosen value held until a forcing event. This block documents the marks, the 2025-26 credit events that tested them (First Brands, Tricolor), the quality deterioration (PIK, negative-FCF borrowers, rising defaults), and - critically - WHO ULTIMATELY HOLDS THE RISK as it migrates into insurance annuities and retail 401(k)s.
metadata.WARNING: EVIDENCE-GRADED. Size, credit events, PIK/default data, and the 401(k) Executive Order are fact. The 'marks are inflated' reading is the strong interpretation of the BDC price-vs-NAV gap (the gap is fact; whether it is inflation or deterioration - both adverse - is the contested part). Apollo's Marc Rowan publicly REJECTS the systemic-risk framing; presented. Kept out of the proofs.
metadata.method: FSB (Vulnerabilities in Private Credit, May 2026), SDNY enforcement statements, Bloomberg (bad-PIK), Goldman Sachs, BDC 10-Qs, iCapital/Dechert valuation pieces, SEC/DOL/White House on retailization, AM Best on insurer related-party lending.
cross_refs: spec-sec-filings-primary.json + models/z3/reflexive_marks.py (self-marked asset class #2 - AI fair-value)
spec-unwind-timing.json (where this lands in a forced unwind)

self_marked_navs.grade: BDC price-NAV GAP is fact; 'inflated marks' interpretation CONTESTED
facts: Private credit ~doubled to ~$2T in five years; loans are MANAGER-VALUED, illiquid, not exchange-priced - the manager earns fees on the very mark it sets.
2025 divergence: the public S&P BDC Index fell ~13% while private (non-traded) BDCs reported ~+8-10% - either portfolio health deteriorated or NAVs are inflated/lagged (both adverse).
Nov 2025: the SDNY US Attorney flagged as an enforcement focus whether private-fund advisers 'cherry-pick prices' that benefit themselves through higher fees (ties spec-sec-sdny-regulatory).
Apollo's Marc Rowan publicly rejects the systemic-risk framing ('people lost their minds over private credit fears') - the counter-view, recorded.

FIRST BRANDS (auto parts): marked at/above 100 cents on the dollar by private-credit funds months before bankruptcy; pitched as a $6B loan opportunity (Jefferies) with ~$1B cash - now top loans ~one-third of value; earlier thread: ~$2.3B receivables 'missing' (macro-firstbrands-ubs).
TRICOLOR (subprime auto): AAA-rated ABS trading at full value before bankruptcy; defaulted on ~$1B; lower bonds crashed to ~12 cents.
Both show the same thing: a chosen/rated value held at par until the forcing event, then a near-instant repricing to a fraction - the mark was fiction, revealed only on default.
PIK (payment-in-kind, interest deferred onto principal) ~8.8% of total investment income in late 2025; public BDCs average ~8% PIK.
'BAD PIK' (deferred mid-loan due to liquidity strain, not structured at origination) ~6.4% of private-credit loans Q4-2025 - nearly TRIPLE 2021 levels.
~40% of private-credit borrowers now have NEGATIVE free cash flow (vs ~25% in 2021).
US private-credit default rate ~5.8% (Jan 2026, up from 5.6% Dec); companies needing unexpected loan-term changes jumped 2.5% -> 6.4% YoY.
FSB issued a report on private-credit vulnerabilities (May 2026) - the global regulator flagging the asset class.

who_holds_the_bag.the_migration: The risk is moving OFF banks and sophisticated funds and INTO insurance reserves (annuities) and retail retirement accounts - the least-able-to-bear, longest-locked-up holders.
facts: INSURANCE-PE NEXUS: Apollo's Athene manages >$300B with a large, growing direct-lending/asset-based-finance allocation; KKR's Global Atlantic similar. AM Best: ~ONE-FIFTH of Athene US Life and Global Atlantic investments are loans to AFFILIATED funds (related-party / self-dealing structure).

Insurance-linked capital deployed ~$180B into private credit in 2025 (up from ~$120B in 2023) - life insurers funding annuities with illiquid manager-marked credit, a shift from their traditional high-grade-bond books.
RETAILIZATION: interval funds / tender-offer funds / BDCs had >$400B assets (end-2024), expected to MORE THAN DOUBLE by 2029; from 2025 privately-offered BDCs can issue multiple retail share classes.

401(k) ACCESS: Trump Executive Order (Aug 7 2025) opens defined-contribution plans to private credit/equity/real estate/crypto; a DOL proposed rule (Mar 2025) broadens alt access; SEC leadership pushing 'democratization' (Better Markets and others call it pushing illiquid, opaquely-marked risk onto Main Street).

edges.notes: Apollo/Blue Owl/PIMCO are the marginal lenders to the AI buildout (Oracle $72B, CoreWeave, Meta Hyperion $27B) - ties spec-sec-filings-primary + macro-cre-privatecredit.
~$180B insurance-linked capital into private credit (2025, up from $120B 2023); ~1/5 of Athene/Global Atlantic investments are loans to AFFILIATED funds - the risk lands in annuity reserves.
Trump EO (Aug 7 2025) + DOL rule open 401(k)s to private credit; interval/BDC retail wrappers >$400B doubling by 2029 - illiquid manager-marked risk to Main Street.

assessment: Private credit is the THIRD self-marked asset class, completing the corpus's epistemic pattern: bank securities held at HTM COST (macro-bank-htm-marks), AI stakes held at FAIR-VALUE/EQUITY marks (reflexive_marks), and private-credit loans held at MANAGER-SET NAVs - all chosen values, not market prices, held until a forcing event. The 2025-26 events tested the marks and they failed instantly: First Brands marked >=100c months before bankruptcy then to ~33c; Tricolor AAA-at-par then 12c. The deterioration is measurable (bad-PIK ~3x 2021, ~40% of borrowers FCF-negative, defaults ~5.8%), and the public-BDC price (-13%) vs private-BDC NAV (+8-10%) gap says the marks lag reality (whether by inflation or lag, both adverse; Rowan's denial noted). The decisive finding is WHERE THE RISK GOES: off banks/funds and INTO insurance annuities (Apollo/Athene, ~1/5 related-party affiliated-fund loans; ~$180B insurance capital in 2025) and RETAIL 401(k)s (Trump EO Aug-2025, BDC retail wrappers doubling) - the longest-locked, least-able-to-bear holders, valued at marks they cannot independently check. This is the same private credit that is the marginal lender to the AI buildout (PIMCO/Apollo/Blue Owl) and the contagion PrivateCredit->Banks leg. Overlay; not used in the proofs; it is the answer to 'who ultimately holds the bag.'
